asset_name: Leaf cluster obstacle — dense canopy lump
shared_properties: The obstacle keeps a warm autumn palette that remains readable over the sky and tree layers.
The silhouette acts as a single rounded blocking mass with a compact lower trunk cue.
Secondary leaf fray stays inside the 80x80px frame in every pose.
differences: This obstacle replaces strict linear geometry with a soft canopy lump to vary the scan pattern.
The animation introduces a gentle sway instead of branch-like horizontal stretching.
Orange highlights make the obstacle feel seasonal while darker pockets keep the collision mass obvious.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas obstacle entity for a subtle sway loop that keeps the canopy lump readable without code-side deformation.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/obstacles_leaf_cluster_dense_4f_7fps.png during stage setup and assign a loop timer for the subtle sway animation frames.
runtime_notes: Use the animated atlas at 7fps for a gentle canopy shimmer that does not alter the collision outline.
Keep the AABB collider anchored to the middle 72x72px region so the outer leaf fray remains a visual cushion.
Spawn this obstacle from 600px depth onward to increase silhouette variety after the earliest training beats.

context.usage: Rounded leaf-mass obstacle used to vary silhouette density after the tutorial zone.

shape.silhouette: Rounded canopy clump with clustered lobes and a darker trunk nub at the lower edge.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 320x80px with 4 frames of 80x80px and 0px spacing.
The silhouette reads as a single obstacle mass from a gameplay zoomed-out view and never breaks into detached pieces.
The animation preserves the outer bounds so the collider can remain constant across all frames.
